stem: A factory produces textile rolls using two sources. Loom Section East handles 66% of production with a known defect rate of 1.1%. Loom Section West handles 34% of production, but its defect rate is uncertain — recent quality audits suggest it lies between 4.1% and 6.6%, depending on raw material batch quality. A randomly selected textile roll is found to be defective. What is the probability it came from Loom Section West?

ground_truth.answer: P(Loom Section West | defective) ranges from 0.658 to 0.756 as Loom Section West's defect rate varies from 4.1% to 6.6%. Point estimate at 5.4%: 0.717.

solution_steps: Apply Bayes' theorem: P(B|def) = P(def|B)×P(B) / [P(def|A)×P(A) + P(def|B)×P(B)].
The interval [0.658, 0.756] captures the range of posterior probabilities.
common_failure_modes: Using only the midpoint defect rate and providing a single answer
Comparing defect rates directly without applying Bayes' theorem
